displayTitle: The Coil Is a Test Parameter
kicker: ET LEVEL II // COIL SHAPE & HALL ELEMENTS

beats.narration: You tuned the part and the settings. One factor is left, and it is the one most often treated as furniture: the test coil itself.

payload.title: THE COIL IS A PARAMETER
payload.sub: Geometry sets the baseline everything moves around.

beats.narration: Geometry writes the empty-coil inductance, and the impedance plane is referenced to it. Double the turns and inductance quadruples.
Eddy currents mirror the winding: zero on the axis, peaking in a ring beneath the copper. Footprint follows diameter.

payload.heading: What the coil actually illuminates
payload.note: ZERO ON AXIS · PEAK UNDER WINDINGS · DEPTH ~ COIL RADIUS

beats.narration: Signal follows the fraction of field disturbed. A small coil makes a crack loud; a large coil averages for conductivity sorting.

payload.heading: Match the footprint to the question

chips.s: Crack fills a big share of a tight field

beats.narration: And the Hall element reads flux density itself — flat to DC, where a coil's output collapses. Match shape and detector to the requirement.

payload.sub: Below the crossover, the Hall element wins.
